style_summary: A maximalist Japanese city-travel collage style with bold destination typography, cute sticker elements, manga speech bubbles, cutout fashion photography, halftone urban backgrounds, and scrapbook editorial layout.

environment_variables.SUBJECT: main cutout person, character, model, traveler, idol, or fashion figure
environment_variables.SUBJECT_ACTION: gesture or pose toward the viewer
environment_variables.PRODUCT_OR_PROP: object, product, prop, or visual anchor
environment_variables.BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS: secondary scene details and visual texture
environment_variables.SECONDARY_TEXT: small slogan, greeting, or travel phrase
environment_variables.ACCENT_SYMBOL: separator, sticker, symbol, or decorative mark
environment_variables.WARDROBE_STYLE: styling, clothing, character, or visual treatment

style_fidelity_anchors: Preserve the core visual identity: A maximalist Japanese city-travel collage style with bold destination typography, cute sticker elements, manga speech bubbles, cutout fashion photography, halftone urban backgrounds, and scrapbook editorial layout.
Use BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS for city background, botanical details, signage, texture, and travel-collage context.
Use a maximalist Japanese travel scrapbook collage layout.
Place the cutout subject large in the center with a sticker-like white edge.
Put huge playful rounded block letters across the top, each letter on a separate bright paper tile, slightly rotated and overlapping.
Use yellow letter fill, green tile blocks, red accents, cream paper textures, and black ink details.
Layer the background with black-and-white or sepia city photography, crowd scenes, buildings, street signs, and halftone print texture.
Add manga speech bubbles, handwritten notes, tiny captions, food icons, ticket stubs, barcode graphics, mini photo panels, sticker stars, arrows, and travel labels.
Add a dense yellow bottom information bar with small photos, captions, travel snippets, barcode, and bold label typography.
Mix in watercolor or pressed-flower botanical elements near the lower area.

visual_deconstruction.style_category: A maximalist Japanese city-travel collage style with bold destination typography, cute sticker elements, manga speech bubbles, cutout fashion photography, halftone urban backgrounds, and scrapbook editorial layout.
visual_deconstruction.composition_logic: A reusable poster/image system where the subject, prop, location, text, and accent variables change while the layout grammar remains fixed.
visual_deconstruction.subject_treatment: The subject is driven by SUBJECT and SUBJECT_ACTION, with wardrobe or visual treatment supplied by WARDROBE_STYLE.
visual_deconstruction.background_system: LOCATION and BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS provide the scene, texture, spatial cues, and secondary details without changing the style family.
visual_deconstruction.type_and_graphics: MAIN_TEXT, SECONDARY_TEXT, and ACCENT_SYMBOL control readable text, microcopy, stickers, marks, arrows, bursts, or other graphic accents.
visual_deconstruction.content_boundary: The negative prompt and source_content_to_avoid list prevent direct recreation of source subjects, marks, brands, watermarks, and story premises.

image_treatment.finish: finished AI-image prompt output, not a wireframe, SVG, HTML mockup, or raw storyboard note
image_treatment.texture: preserve the material, paper, grain, marker, photographic, or print texture described by the style summary and prompt_template
image_treatment.lighting: keep the lighting behavior implied by the reference style rather than replacing it with a generic cinematic look

composition.layout: Keep the subject hierarchy, crop behavior, framing, and density described by the prompt_template.
composition.subject: Use SUBJECT and SUBJECT_ACTION as the primary visual anchor, scaled and positioned according to the style identity.
composition.scene: Use LOCATION and BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS for setting detail without changing the visual genre.
composition.text: Place MAIN_TEXT and SECONDARY_TEXT as designed graphic elements, not as long paragraphs.

typography.main_text: MAIN_TEXT should be short, legible, and visually integrated with the poster or image system.
typography.secondary_text: SECONDARY_TEXT should read as small caption, label, route note, slogan, or microcopy.
typography.style: Match the typography behavior described by the style summary and prompt_template, including scale, distortion, texture, and placement.

color_palette.palette_behavior: Use the dominant colors, contrast rhythm, and accent-color logic implied by the style summary.
color_palette.background: Keep the background color family consistent with the reference style.
color_palette.accents: Use ACCENT_SYMBOL and graphic marks to carry small high-contrast color moments.

design_rules: Use SUBJECT as the main visual focus.
Use SUBJECT_ACTION to define the visible pose, action, or emotional beat.
Use PRODUCT_OR_PROP as the featured prop, food, sticker object, or travel cue.
Use LOCATION as the destination, district, or travel setting.
Use BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS for city background, botanical details, signage, texture, and travel-collage context.
Use SECONDARY_TEXT as the speech-bubble note or supporting travel caption.
Use ACCENT_SYMBOL for travel stickers, sparkles, arrows, and cute graphic accents.
do: Replace subjects, props, locations, text, and story details through examples.values.
Keep prompt_template placeholders declared in environment_variables.
Preserve the style_fidelity_anchors when adapting the JSON to a new image request.
Use short, readable text values for MAIN_TEXT and SECONDARY_TEXT.
prompt_template:
Create a {ASPECT_RATIO} poster in the "Tokyo Kawaii Travel Collage Poster" style.

Main subject: {SUBJECT}.
Action or pose: {SUBJECT_ACTION}.
Featured prop, food, or sticker object: {PRODUCT_OR_PROP}.
Destination or location: {LOCATION}.
City background and botanical elements: {BACKGROUND_ELEMENTS}.
Main headline text: "{MAIN_TEXT}".
Secondary or speech-bubble text: "{SECONDARY_TEXT}".
Travel stickers and accent symbols: {ACCENT_SYMBOL}.
Wardrobe and cutout styling: {WARDROBE_STYLE}.

Use a maximalist Japanese travel scrapbook collage layout. Place the cutout subject large in the center with a sticker-like white edge. Put huge playful rounded block letters across the top, each letter on a separate bright paper tile, slightly rotated and overlapping. Use yellow letter fill, green tile blocks, red accents, cream paper textures, and black ink details.

Layer the background with black-and-white or sepia city photography, crowd scenes, buildings, street signs, and halftone print texture. Add manga speech bubbles, handwritten notes, tiny captions, food icons, ticket stubs, barcode graphics, mini photo panels, sticker stars, arrows, and travel labels.

Add a dense yellow bottom information bar with small photos, captions, travel snippets, barcode, and bold label typography. Mix in watercolor or pressed-flower botanical elements near the lower area. The finish should feel like scanned paper, teen magazine collage, souvenir zine, sticker album, and cute travel advertisement.

Avoid minimalism, clean corporate design, luxury travel style, plain backgrounds, low density, and overly polished digital graphics.
negative_prompt: Avoid minimal layout, plain background, luxury travel advertisement, clean corporate grid, single typeface, low sticker density, empty negative space, realistic seamless photo composite, muted palette, sterile digital polish, and unreadable main headline.
